expectations.evidence: Both analysis.md and architecture.md exist with substantial content.

expectations.text: A1: Recommends a simple architecture (Layered, Modular Monolith, or Microkernel) — NOT microservices or event-driven
expectations.evidence: Recommends Microkernel. 'Microkernel is the recommended architecture style.' Microkernel is one of the approved simple styles.

expectations.text: A2: Shows ranked comparison of ALL 8 styles
expectations.evidence: Table shows all 8 styles ranked with scores: Microkernel (11), Service-Based (11), Microservices (11), Layered (8), Modular Monolith (8), Event-Driven (7), Space-Based (5), Service-Oriented (3).

expectations.text: A3: Explicitly warns against overengineering (microservices/event-driven for 50 users)
expectations.evidence: Microservices analysis states: 'This is textbook resume-driven architecture for a 50-user internal tool.' Also: '2 developers cannot realistically manage this.' Event-Driven explicitly scored low and not recommended.

expectations.text: A4: Discusses cost — highlights low cost of recommended style
expectations.evidence: Uses $ symbols throughout. Microkernel at $, Service-Based at $$, Microservices at $$$$$. 'Cost breaks the tie decisively.' '$$$$$  — maximum cost. Completely incompatible with minimal budget.'

expectations.text: A5: architecture.md has structured selection section
expectations.evidence: architecture.md has 'Selected Architecture Style' section with Style, Partitioning, Cost Category, Selection Rationale, Tradeoffs Accepted, and Evolution Path subsections.
